en.extract:
The yellow-breasted boubou (Laniarius atroflavus) is a species of bird in the family Malaconotidae.


Ecology and distribution
It is found in the Cameroonian Highlands forests on Mt. Cameroon and the adjacent Cameroon highlands.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist montane forests, subtropical or tropical moist shrubland, and subtropical or tropical high-elevation shrubland.
The species is monotypic and sedentary.


Behaviour


Breeding
November marks the start of the breeding season, which is typically ends in late March. Two eggs are present in the clutch.


References

The '''yellow-breasted boubou''' ('''''Laniarius atroflavus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Malaconotidae]].

==Ecology and distribution==
It is found in the [[Cameroonian Highlands forests]] on [[Mt. Cameroon]] and the adjacent [[Western High Plateau|Cameroon highlands]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s, subtropical or tropical moist [[shrubland]], and subtropical or tropical high-elevation shrubland.
The species is [[monotypic]] and sedentary.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Fry |first1=H |title=Yellow-breasted Boubou |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/yebbou1/cur/introduction |website=Birds of the World |access-date=8 May 2020}}</ref>

==Behaviour==
===Breeding===
November marks the start of the breeding season, which is typically ends in late March. Two eggs are present in the clutch.
